misconceptions.display_name: Counts transfers and used goods in GDP
misconceptions.description: Believes transfer payments, sales of used goods, or intermediate goods add to GDP. GDP counts only final goods and services newly produced this year, so transfers and resales are excluded.
trigger_phrases: intermediate goods are part of gdp

misconceptions.display_name: Uses gross instead of net (forgets depreciation)
misconceptions.description: Forgets to subtract depreciation, treating gross national product as if it were net national product, or otherwise conflates GDP, GNP, and NNP. Net measures subtract the capital worn out over the year.
trigger_phrases: gross is the same as net
